title: Chapter 77
text: OH LET ME NOT BE PROUD Oh let me not be proud, dear Lord, Nor rank myself above The other Primates, through whose genes We grew into your Love. A million million years, Your Days, Your methods past discerning, Yet through Your blend of DNA s Came passion, mind, and learning. We cannot always trace Your path Through Monkey and Gorilla, Yet all are sheltered underneath Your Heavenly Umbrella. And if we vaunt and puff ourselves With vanity and pride, Recall Australopithecus, Our Animal inside. So keep us far from worser traits, Aggression, anger, greed; Let us not scorn our lowly birth, Nor yet our Primate seed. From The God’s Gardeners Oral Hymnbook
